name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: ICYMI: In-person polling places and vote centers are available for those who need them, even during #COVID19. Take advantage of early voting if your state allows it to avoid crowds on Election Day.

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: What should you do if your ballot doesn’t arrive? Don’t wait – if you want to #VoteByMail during #COVID19, make sure to call your local elections office and follow up on your request for an absentee ballot.

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: Did you know there are faster ways to return your ballot when you #VoteByMail? If your state has secure designated locations for drop-off, using these will help to ensure that your ballot is received in time to be counted on Election Day.

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: Did you know that your ballot might be rejected if you use an “X” rather filling in a circle? Did you know some ballots have witness or notary requirements? Read the directions on your ballot carefully to make sure your vote counts!

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: @CapehartJ @MSNBC @amjoyshow awesome

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: It might take longer to deliver all #VoteByMail ballots – so this year, be sure to request, complete, and return your ballot as early as possible to make your voice heard. Be sure your ballot is signed, sealed, and delivered!

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: Be sure your vote is counted by casting your ballot early, either by mail or in-person. If voting by mail, request your ballot early and return it promptly!

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: How to make sure your vote gets counted? Vote early – either by mail or in person! Check out your state’s policy on #VoteByMail or early in-person voting, and make sure you have a plan to cast your ballot safely during #COVID19.

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: Americans who wish to #VoteByMail can take additional steps to make sure their vote will count: Request your ballot Read all instructions Sign your ballot Seal your ballot Mail it back or drop it off ...and do it early!

name: Rep. Stacey Plaskett
tweet: The USPS is a pillar of our democracy, enshrined in the Constitution and essential for providing critical services: delivering prescriptions, Social Security checks, paychecks, tax returns and absentee ballots to millions of Americans, including in our most remote communities.

tweet: Public safety officers put their lives on the line every day to protect us from serious threats. Whether they are law enforcement officers, firefighters, or emergency medical technicians, public safety officers selflessly charge into danger zones when others are in need.

tweet: During #COVID19, public safety officers are bravely answering the call of duty. While our citizens are doing their best to engage in social distancing, public safety officers remain on the job, seeking to protect and heal us in ways that may lead them to be exposed to the virus.

tweet: Millions of American families rely on small businesses for their livelihoods. Any effort to protect our economy must prioritize these local leaders. #FamiliesFirst
